extraction.one_line_summary: NIZZOLO Giacomo won a Verona sprint on his 17th Giro podium, closing down AFFINI Edoardo's late solo flyer in the final 15 meters.
tactical_patterns: AFFINI Edoardo's time trial engine nearly stole sprint stage breaking clear with 1km remaining, TT specialists viable late attackers.
Belgian opportunism from DE BONDT at 50km woke peloton but teams intentionally slowed to delay break catch, energy management over tempo.
GAVIRIA Fernando contested intermediate sprints despite not winning, points jersey battles drain fast finishers before decisive finale sprint.

rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: positive: broke 16-podium winless drought with explosive finish close, form suggests improved top-end speed after pattern of near-misses
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: positive: TT specialist nearly held off pure sprinters from 1km out, exceptionally strong final kilometer power reserves
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: finished third maintaining points jersey, tactical positioning working but lacks explosive speed for stage wins
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: contested intermediate sprints but only fifth in finale, spending energy early compromising final sprint outcome
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: required AFFINI leadout but couldn't follow late attack, dependent positioning reveals lack of independent sprint initiation
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: home roads in Verona but failed to feature in sprint, emotional distraction from Olympic flag-bearer announcement likely factor
